data.title: Meta to share AI infrastructure costs via $2 billion asset sale
data.description:
Key points:
  • Move aims to bring in partners for data center co-development
  • Shift reflects growing trend among tech giants to share AI infrastructure costs
  • $2.04 bln in land and construction assets reclassified as held-for-sale

Meta Platforms is pressing ahead with efforts to bring in outside partners to help fund the massive infrastructure needed to power artificial intelligence, disclosing plans in a filing on Thursday to offload $2 billion in data center assets as part of that strategy.

The strategy reflects a broader shift among tech giants — long known for self-funding growth — as they grapple with the soaring cost of building and powering data centers to support generative AI.

The social media giant said earlier this week that it was exploring ways to work with financial partners to co-develop data centers to help finance its massive capital outlay for next year.

“We’re exploring ways to work with financial partners to co-develop data centers,” Meta Chief Finance Officer Susan Li said on a post-earnings conference call on Wednesday.

While the company still expects to fund much of its capital spending internally, some projects could attract “significant external financing” and offer more flexibility if infrastructure needs shift over time, Li said.

The company did not have any finalized transactions to announce, she said.

The disclosure in Meta's quarterly filing, however, signals that plans are firming up.

In its quarterly filing on Thursday, Meta said it had approved a plan in June to dispose of certain data center assets and reclassified $2.04 billion worth of land and construction-in-progress as "held-for-sale".

These assets were expected to be contributed to a third party within the next twelve months for co-developing data centers.

Meta did not record a loss on the reclassification, which values the assets at the lower of their carrying amounts or fair value less costs to sell. As of June 30, total held-for-sale assets stood at $3.26 billion, according to the filing.

Meta declined to comment for this story.

CEO Mark Zuckerberg has laid out plans to invest hundreds of billions of dollars into constructing AI data center “superclusters” for superintelligence.

“Just one of these covers a significant part of the footprint of Manhattan,” he said.

The Instagram and WhatsApp owner on Wednesday raised the bottom end of its annual capital expenditures forecast by $2 billion, to $66 billion to $72 billion.

It reported stronger-than-expected ad sales, boosted by AI-driven improvements to targeting and content delivery. Executives said those gains were helping offset rising infrastructure costs tied to its long-term AI push.

data.title: Appian, Resideo, Bitfarms, and More Stocks See Action From Activist Investors — Barrons.com
data.description:
These disclosures are from 13Ds filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. 13Ds are filed within 10 days of an entity's attaining more than 5% in any class of a company's securities. Subsequent changes in holdings or intentions must be reported in amended filings. This material is from July 24 through July 30, 2025. Source: VerityData

Increases in Holdings

Resideo Technologies (REZI) CD&R Channel Holdings II raised its stake in the smart home and security technology company to 28,750,250 shares. CD&R did so through the purchase of 4,477,919 Resideo Technologies shares pursuant to a Master Confirmation Agreement on July 25, at a price per share equal to $22.33.

Following the latest purchases, CD&R Channel Holdings owns 17.2% of Resideo Technologies' outstanding stock. Shares of Resideo Technologies have gained roughly 25.2% in value since the beginning of this year.

ZimVie (ZIMV) Gamco Investors increased its stake in the dental implant device company to 1,402,775 shares. Gamco Investors did so through the purchase of 461,421 ZimVie shares from May 29 through July 25, at per share prices ranging from $8.48 to $18.76, and partially offset those purchases through the sale of 36,449 ZimVie shares from May 30 to July 23 at $8.43 to $18.77 apiece.

Following the latest activity, Gamco Investors now owns 5% of ZimVie's outstanding stock. Shares of ZimVie have gained approximately 36.5% in value since the beginning of this year.

Decreases in Holdings

Appian (APPN) Abdiel Capital Advisors lowered its stake in the business process management platform to 5,690,592 shares. Abdiel Capital Advisors did so through the sale of 748,338 Appian shares from July 23 through July 25, at per share prices ranging from $28.53 to $30.42, after selling another 589,917 Appian shares from July 10 to July 22 at $28.40 to $31.09 apiece.

Following the latest sales, Abdiel Capital Advisors continues to own 13.2% of Appian's outstanding stock. Shares of Appian have lost approximately 13.7% of their value since the beginning of this year.

Bitfarms (BITF) Riot Platforms reduced its stake in the Bitcoin-mining company to 46,421,327 shares. Riot Platforms did so through the sale of 8,323,736 Bitfarms shares from July 18 through July 23, at per share prices ranging from $1.10 to $1.34.

Following the latest sales, Riot Platforms continues to own 8.3% of Bitfarms' outstanding stock. Shares of Bitfarms have lost roughly 25.5% in value since the beginning of this year.

Satellogic (SATL) Hannover Holdings lowered its stake in the satellite imagery provider to 5,194,043 shares. Hannover Holdings did so through the sale of 933,222 Satellogic shares from June 6 through July 23, at per share prices ranging from $3.52 to $3.88.

Following the latest sales, Hannover Holdings continues to own 5.7% of Satellogic's outstanding stock. Shares of Satellogic have gained approximately 10.8% in value since the beginning of this year.

Email: editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Judge blocks Trump from fast-tracking deportations of immigrants paroled into US
data.description:
A federal judge on Friday blocked President Donald Trump's administration from fast-tracking the deportation of potentially hundreds of thousands of immigrants who were paroled into the U.S. under former President Joe Biden's humanitarian programs.

U.S. District Judge Jia Cobb in Washington, D.C. said it served the public interest to put on hold the Department of Homeland Security's expedited removals for those who entered with temporary parole, rather than cause irreparable harm to immigrants by allowing them.

Cobb also quoted a recent dissenting opinion by Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor, in rejecting DHS' assertion of a substantial interest in carrying out Trump's policies, including the prompt removal of immigrants not entitled to stay.

"This case presents a question of fair play," Cobb wrote. "This court will not endorse the radical proposition that the President is harmed, irreparably, whenever he cannot do something he wants to do, even if what he wants to do is break the law."

Cobb was appointed to the bench by Biden, a Democrat.

Trump, a Republican, has sought to deport record numbers of immigrants, but complained that courts and existing laws get in the way.

In an emailed statement, Homeland Security spokesperson Tricia McLaughlin accused Cobb of ignoring a Supreme Court precedent allowing expedited removals. "This ruling is lawless and won't stand," she said.

Expedited removals could allow immigrant parolees to be deported without hearings or lawyers.

The lawsuit challenging them had been brought by three immigrant rights groups whose members included parolees subject to that process.

"Over the last few months, immigrants who entered the country with parole have been living a nightmare," Ama Frimpong, legal director of one of the groups, CASA, said in a statement. "They can now sleep a little easier with the relief, however temporary it is, that this court decision has offered."

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers have in recent months arrested immigrants at courthouses, following a legal maneuver where immigration judges dropped people's cases so that they could be placed in expedited removal.

The case is Coalition for Humane Immigrant Rights et al v Noem et al, U.S. District Court, District of Columbia, No. 25-00872.

data.title: Trump Media & Technology 2Q Loss $20M >DJT
On Aug. 6, Kodiak Gas Services to Replace NV5 Global in Index; NV5 to Be Sold to Acuren
Materials Down After Weak Jobs Data, Copper Rout — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell after weak jobs data and tariff moves that caused a plunge for copper prices.

Copper futures tumbled 23%, $4.41 per pound, this week for their largest weekly decline on record, going back to 1968.

The tariff levied on copper imports was not as broad as feared.

On the eve of the Trump administration's tariff deadline, stores of the industrial metal in the U.S. were priced at a 30% premium to London copper, as arbitrageurs moved metal across the Atlantic to play the spread in prices.

Trump cited Canada's retaliation to his April warnings when he signed an executive order raising tariffs to 35% from 25% on roughly 15% of Canadian imports that aren't compliant with the USMCA trade agreement.

Gold futures ticked up as traders sought out safe havens.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: ROPER TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Roper Technologies Inc., a diversified technology company specializing in software, engineered products, and solutions for various industries, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, driven by strategic acquisitions and organic growth across its business segments.

Financial Highlights

Roper Technologies Inc. reported robust financial performance for the three months ended June 30, 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Net Revenues: $1,943.6 million, an increase of 13.2% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by acquisition contributions and organic growth across segments.
  • Gross Profit: $1,345.4 million, reflecting a gross margin of 69.2%, slightly down from 69.5% in the same period in 2024 due to revenue mix changes.
  • Income from Operations: $548.3 million, with an operating margin of 28.2%, compared to 28.8% in the same period in 2024, impacted by higher compensation and acquisition-related expenses.
  • Net Earnings: $378.3 million, representing 19.5% of net revenues, slightly down from 19.6% in the same period in 2024, due to increased interest expenses and tax rates.
  • Diluted EPS: $3.49, compared to $3.12 in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved earnings performance and share repurchases.

Business Highlights

Roper Technologies Inc. also reported significant operational achievements and strategic initiatives during the second quarter of 2025:

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Application Software segment saw a 17.5% increase in net revenues, driven by acquisitions such as Transact and CentralReach, with organic growth led by businesses in acute healthcare, property and casualty insurance, and legal markets.
    • Network Software segment revenues grew by 5.8%, with organic growth in construction, freight match, and alternate site healthcare markets.
    • Technology Enabled Products segment revenues increased by 10.1%, led by medical products and water meter technology businesses.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued positive cash flows from operating activities, which will support debt reduction. However, geopolitical and economic uncertainties, such as inflation, supply chain disruptions, and labor shortages, could impact business prospects. The company maintains an active acquisition program, though future acquisitions depend on various factors, including market conditions.

SEC Filing: ROPER TECHNOLOGIES INC [ ROP ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: OneWater Marine Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
OneWater Marine Inc., a leading marine retailer specializing in the sale of new and pre-owned boats, financing and insurance products, and marine-related parts and accessories, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, reflecting both the challenges and opportunities faced in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $552.9 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, driven by strong pre-owned boat sales.
  • Gross Profit: $128.7 million, indicating a stable margin despite increased cost of sales.
  • Income from Operations: $30.4 million, a decrease from the prior year, impacted by higher selling, general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income: $10.7 million, down from $16.7 million in the previous year, primarily due to increased interest expenses.
  • Net Earnings Per Share of Class A common stock – diluted: $0.65, compared to $0.99 in the prior year, reflecting the impact of reduced net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through two main segments: Dealerships and Distribution. The Dealerships segment engages in the sale of new and pre-owned boats, financing and insurance products, repairs and maintenance services, and offers marine-related parts and accessories. The Distribution segment focuses on the manufacturing, assembly, and distribution of marine-related products to distributors, big box retailers, and online retailers.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates 97 retail locations and 9 distribution centers across 19 states, with a strong presence in the Southeast, Gulf Coast, Mid-Atlantic, and Northeast regions. These areas are among the top twenty states for marine retail expenditures, indicating a strategic focus on high-demand markets.
  • Sales Units: The company reported a decrease in new boat sales revenue, while pre-owned boat sales saw an increase, reflecting a shift in consumer purchasing behavior. This trend suggests a growing market for pre-owned boats.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in the marine retail sector, driven by strategic acquisitions and expansion into high-demand markets. The company is also evaluating the impact of recent legislative changes, such as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, on its operations and financial performance.

SEC Filing: OneWater Marine Inc. [ ONEW ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: Shareholders Alert: Investigation Into Duolingo, Inc. (DOUL) - Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Protect Your Rights
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 1, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Duolingo, Inc. ("Duolingo, Inc.") (NASDAQ:DOUL) concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On July 28, 2025, JMP Securities lowered its price target on Duolingo stock to $450 from $475, citing concerns about slowing user engagement ahead of the Company's second-quarter earnings report. Specifically, third party data suggested that daily active user growth decelerated to approximately 39% year-over-year in the second quarter, a noticeable drop from about 51% in the first quarter.

Following this news, Duolingo's stock price fell over 6% on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/duolingo-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=159346&wire=1&utm_campaign=62

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Cooling Job Market Opens Door to September Cut Despite Inflation Jitters | Federal ...
data.description:
Cooling Job Market Opens Door to September Cut Despite Inflation Jitters

A key question for the Fed will be whether the economy is weakening or whether a recent slowdown in job growth reflects temporary factors.

----

Federal Reserve Governor Adriana Kugler to Resign

Kugler's departure creates an opportunity for President Trump to nominate a potential successor to Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

----

Trump Tariffs Are Here. The Road Ahead Still Isn't Clear.

Even the most fleshed-out trade pacts are light on details, and in some cases there are different interpretations of what was agreed upon.

----

A Weak Jobs Report and Revised Tariff Plans Send Markets Reeling

U.S. stocks and bond yields fall sharply on Friday, and investors are betting a Fed interest-rate cut is coming soon.

----

U.S. Hiring Slowed Sharply Over the Summer

The latest month's report â€‹suggests that pockets of weakness in the labor market are starting to take hold. Revisions showed employers added a combined 258,000 fewer jobs in May and June than estimated.

----

U.S. Factory Activity Continues to Contract

The Institute for Supply Management said its purchasing managers' index of manufacturing activity contracted to 48 in July from 49 in June.

----

Eurozone Inflation Holds Steady at Target, Leaving ECB in 'Good Place'

Consumer prices in July were 2.0% higher than the same month of last year, matching the inflation rate of June.

----

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. ISM Services Data in Focus; BOE Expected to Cut Rates

The key ISM survey of activity in the U.S. services sector will be closely watched as investors continue to gauge the likelihood of U.S. interest-rate cuts in the coming weeks.

----

China's Manufacturing Activity Tumbles Into Contraction

A private gauge of China's manufacturing activity returned to contractionary territory in July, as softening new business growth led factories to scale back production.

----

Asia Manufacturing Outlook Marks Five-Year Low as Tariff Concerns Linger

July's S&P manufacturing PMI Future Output Index-which measures expectations regarding output in the year ahead- showed subdued confidence among goods producers in Asia.

----

Businesses Continue to Face Uncertainty After Latest U.S. Tariff Blitz, ICC Says

Businesses around the world are still seeking clarity on the barriers they will face as exporters to the U.S., with many pausing big decisions on investment and hiring, according to the deputy head of the International Chamber of Commerce.

----

Why the Latest Meme Stock Craze Won't Take Down the Overall Market

Krispy Kreme, Opendoor, Rocket and Kohl's are merely the public face of a summer boom in bets by private traders.

----

Buying a Swiss Watch in America Is About to Get a Lot More Expensive

High tariffs threaten demand in the U.S., which was a bright spot for the struggling industry.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 5 PM ET: Boston Beer Founder Jim Koch to Return as CEO | Jury ...
data.description:
Boston Beer Founder Jim Koch to Return as CEO

Michael Spillane, who is currently helming the brewer, plans to step down this month due to personal reasons.

----

Jury Says Tesla Must Pay $243 Million Over Fatal Autopilot Crash

The automaker was found partly liable over its driver-assistance software in a deadly 2019 collision.

----

Figma Had a Dazzling IPO. It Could Have Been $3 Billion Better.

Investors and bankers are wondering why the software company didn't price its shares higher to capitalize on fervent demand.

----

Exxon Sees M&A Possibilities As Oil Giants Post Lower Profits

Exxon Mobil CEO Darren Woods said the oil giant is looking for acquisition opportunities in the oil-and-gas space after losing its challenge to Chevron's $53 billion deal to buy Hess.

----

Colgate Plans More Price Increases to Offset Slower Sales, Higher Costs

Colgate-Palmolive said it needs to further raise prices and adjust product sizes to hit the low end of its full-year organic sales outlook, as consumers remain cautious amid a volatile environment.

----

Perella Weinberg Strikes Deal for Advisory Firm Specializing in Red-Hot Market

The boutique investment bank is buying Devon Park Advisors, a big player in continuation-fund deals.

----

IAG Operating Profit Beats Consensus on Strong Revenue Growth

The British Airways owner said however that it expects underlying costs to increase due to lower capacity growth and higher resilience costs.

----

Nintendo Gets Profit Boost From Strong Switch 2 Sales

The videogame maker said it had sold more than 6 million units of the console since its early June launch and is now boosting production due to strong demand.

----

Moderna Logs Sales Beat Despite Declining Vaccine Demand

Moderna's second-quarter sales fell less sharply than Wall Street expected as it works to expand its offerings amid weakening demand for its Covid-19 vaccine.

----

Kimberly-Clark Sales Dip but Adjusted Profit Above Views

Kimberly-Clark's second-quarter adjusted profit came in above Wall Street estimates but sales fell short of analysts' expectations, driven mostly by divestitures and business exits.

----

Newell Brands Revises 2025 Outlook, Quarterly Sales Decline

Newell Brands, maker of Yankee Candle and Sharpie pens, has revised its outlook for the year to better account for the cost of tariffs.

----

Why Amazon's Massive Retail Business Is No Longer Enough

The accelerating cloud growth at Microsoft and Google points to artificial-intelligence momentum that AWS doesn't show yet.

----

Etsy Turns From TV Ads Toward Search, With AI as the Wild Card

The online seller is making a long-term bet on fashion marketplace Depop as the company's overall buyers continue to drop.

----

Intel Chip-Packaging Expert Takes Job at Samsung

Gang Duan, who was named Intel's inventor of the year in 2024, has left the U.S. company and taken a job at Samsung's components business.

----

Universal Music Shares Fall on Disappointing Earnings and Cash

Investors had high hopes following a strong first quarter but its latest earnings and free cash flow missed expectations.

data.title: Contact Levi & Korsinsky by September 26, 2025 to Join Class Action Against Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT)
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 1, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Lockheed Martin Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/lockheed-martin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=159345&wire=1&utm_campaign=10

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Lockheed Martin Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between January 23, 2024 and July 21, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) Lockheed Martin lacked effective internal controls regarding its purportedly risk adjusted contracts including the reporting of its risk adjusted profit booking rate; (2) Lockheed Martin lacked effective procedures to perform reasonably accurate comprehensive reviews of program requirements, technical complexities, schedule, and risks; (3) Lockheed Martin overstated its ability to deliver on its contract commitments in terms of cost, quality and schedule; (4) as a result, the Company was reasonably likely to report significant losses; and (5) as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Lockheed Martin Corporation stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/lockheed-martin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=159345&wire=1&utm_campaign=10 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Global Medical REIT Inc expected to post earnings of 3 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Global Medical REIT Inc , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Bethesda Maryland-based company is expected to report a 3.3% increase in revenue to $35.373 million from $34.24 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Global Medical REIT Inc is for earnings of 3 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Global Medical REIT Inc is $8.75, about 22.9% above its last closing price of $6.75
